seeds.qualitative_claim: An investment may only proceed to analysis if the analyst can explain in plain language how the business makes money, who its customers are, and why those customers will continue to buy
seeds.qualitative_rule: Reject any investment where the analyst cannot articulate — without jargon, technology trend extrapolation, or industry-insider assumptions — the business model, the source of customer demand, and a plausible long-run trajectory
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit investment in complex industries; prohibits investment in any business where the analyst's understanding is shallow or borrowed rather than genuine
seeds.rationale: Buffett's circle of competence is self-defined and defended with unusual rigidity — he passed on entire sectors including technology and biotech under heavy social pressure. Genuine understanding is operationally defined as the ability to explain the business simply and predict its state a decade out with real confidence.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A target business must possess an identifiable structural competitive advantage that protects its returns on capital from competitive erosion
seeds.qualitative_rule: Reject businesses where no identifiable source of durable competitive advantage — brand power, cost leadership, network effects, switching costs, or regulatory barriers — can be articulated and supported by observable business economics
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require the moat to be impenetrable or permanent; requires only that a meaningful structural advantage exists and can be identified with specificity
seeds.rationale: Moat analysis is the single most important qualitative factor in Buffett's mature framework. Without a structural competitive advantage, high returns on capital will be competed away over time, destroying the foundation for long-term compounding.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A competitive moat that is demonstrably narrowing on a secular basis is a disqualifying condition, even if the moat currently appears wide
seeds.qualitative_rule: If evidence shows the competitive position has been systematically losing ground — through declining market share, pricing power erosion, or successful competitive encroachment confirmed across multiple years — reject the investment regardless of the apparent current moat width
seeds.anti_scope: Does not apply to temporary, cyclical competitive pressure; applies only to structural, secular moat erosion confirmed over a multi-year period and supported by a credible mechanism of disruption
seeds.rationale: Buffett's largest analytical failures — newspapers and IBM — involved moats that were real at purchase but eroded faster than expected. The critical diagnostic is not whether the moat exists today but whether it is widening or narrowing. A real but narrowing moat is a liability, not an asset, over a long holding horizon.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The ability to raise prices without losing meaningful customer volume is the primary operational litmus test of whether a real economic moat exists
seeds.qualitative_rule: Assess whether the business has historically raised prices at or above input cost inflation while maintaining or growing unit volume over a full business cycle; if structural pricing power is absent, any moat claim requires extraordinary additional justification
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require prices to rise every year; assesses the structural capacity to raise prices across a cycle without triggering material volume destruction
seeds.rationale: Buffett stated to the Financial Crisis Inquiry Commission in 2010 that pricing power is more important than good management. He considers it the definitive operational test of whether a competitive moat is real. Coca-Cola's demonstrated ability to raise prices globally while sustaining volume was central to his 1988 investment thesis.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Management that lacks verifiable integrity is disqualifying regardless of business quality or valuation attractiveness
seeds.qualitative_rule: If management has a documented history of misleading shareholders, manipulating reported figures, or structuring transactions to benefit insiders at owner expense, reject the investment and do not reconsider on the basis of other merits
seeds.anti_scope: Does not apply to operational errors, poor capital allocation decisions, or strategic mistakes; applies specifically and exclusively to integrity failures and demonstrated dishonesty toward owners
seeds.rationale: Buffett has stated that intelligence and energy without integrity will destroy you. The Tesco accounting fraud and Salomon Brothers misconduct cases demonstrate this as a hard veto. Dishonest management converts what appears to be a business asset into a concealed liability that is typically discovered at the worst possible moment.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Management that retains earnings without creating equivalent or greater market value over time is destroying shareholder capital through deficient capital allocation
seeds.qualitative_rule: For every dollar of earnings cumulatively retained over a multi-year period, at least one dollar of incremental market capitalization should have been created; a persistent shortfall indicates management should be returning capital to owners rather than retaining it
seeds.anti_scope: Does not apply over short measurement windows or at cyclical troughs; applies to multi-year cumulative patterns only and is a diagnostic flag requiring further investigation, not an automatic rejection trigger
seeds.rationale: Buffett's retained earnings test is his specific, actionable capital allocation quality filter — it directly connects management decision-making to shareholder outcomes and operationalizes the distinction between value-creating retention and value-destroying empire-building.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The investment entity must not use conventional borrowed money that creates exposure to margin calls, fixed maturity dates, or forced liquidation
seeds.qualitative_rule: Prohibit structural use of margin accounts, leveraged instruments, or debt-financed investment positions at the portfolio entity level; any capital structure element that can compel selling at unfavorable prices is categorically excluded
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit portfolio companies from carrying operational debt appropriate to their business; does not prohibit float from insurance operations; restricts only leverage at the investment entity level that introduces forced-sale risk
seeds.rationale: Buffett views conventional debt as structurally fragile — it introduces margin calls, maturity dates, and forced liquidation risk at precisely the worst times. The float model achieves leverage-like return amplification without this fragility. This explains what otherwise appears contradictory: constant warnings against leverage from a man running one of the most leveraged capital structures in finance.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Selling a position requires affirmative, articulable justification; the default state for a high-conviction holding is to hold indefinitely
seeds.qualitative_rule: A sell decision must be justified by at least one of: (a) confirmed secular deterioration of the business's competitive moat, (b) discovered management integrity failure, (c) a materially superior capital redeployment opportunity that exceeds the full tax cost of sale, or (d) price at a gross premium to any reasonable intrinsic value estimate; the absence of a strong fresh buy thesis alone does not constitute justification for selling
seeds.anti_scope: Does not mean positions can never be sold; establishes that selling is the exception requiring explicit cause, not a default response to price weakness, macro uncertainty, or peer pressure
seeds.rationale: Buffett's stated holding period is 'forever.' Truly great businesses become more valuable compounding over time. He has held Coca-Cola for over three decades including through periods of meaningful overvaluation, and has explained that the franchise's continuity made patience economically rational.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The permanent compounding cost of realizing a capital gain must be explicitly weighed before any sell decision on a position with significant embedded unrealized gains
seeds.qualitative_rule: Before finalizing a sell recommendation on a position with material unrealized gains, estimate the after-tax compounding base permanently lost to the tax event and compare it to the expected incremental benefit from redeployment; proceed only if the redeployment benefit materially and durably exceeds this permanent cost
seeds.anti_scope: Does not apply to positions with minimal embedded gains or to loss positions; does not restrict tax-loss harvesting; applies specifically to positions where capital gains taxation would represent a material permanent reduction in the long-run compounding base
seeds.rationale: Buffett has held Coca-Cola through periods of overvaluation explicitly because the tax cost of selling and rebuying would destroy more long-run value than patience costs. After-tax compounding math pervades his entire approach and distinguishes him from investors who optimize on a pre-tax basis.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Business value must be assessed on owner earnings rather than on reported accounting earnings
seeds.qualitative_rule: Define owner earnings as reported net income plus depreciation and amortization, minus the maintenance capital expenditures required to sustain competitive position and unit volume; use this figure as the primary earnings base for all valuation work, not GAAP net income
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit using reported earnings as a starting input; requires adjustment for maintenance capex to arrive at the economically meaningful owner earnings figure before any valuation conclusion is drawn
seeds.rationale: Buffett defined owner earnings in his 1986 shareholder letter as the true measure of a business's cash-generation capacity. GAAP earnings can diverge materially from owner earnings in capital-intensive businesses. The Coca-Cola case is instructive: its asset-light model meant virtually all earnings growth translated directly to free cash flow, a distinction invisible in GAAP income.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Formal multi-year DCF spreadsheet models must not serve as the primary source of valuation conviction
seeds.qualitative_rule: Primary valuation must be conducted as a mental model: estimate normalized owner earnings, assess a conservative long-run growth rate, compare the implied purchase-price return against the required return threshold; a formal spreadsheet with multi-year projections, explicit terminal value, and WACC is prohibited as the primary valuation method
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit quantitative cross-checking or sanity validation; prohibits treating spreadsheet model output as the primary source of conviction rather than as a check on mental reasoning
seeds.rationale: Buffett has explicitly stated he does not build multi-year projections with terminal values and WACC. Precision must reside in the quality of thinking, not in the false confidence of spreadsheet arithmetic — a model producing precise wrong answers is worse than a rough correct one.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Full qualitative assessment of business quality and competitive durability must be completed and confirmed before examining whether the price is attractive
seeds.qualitative_rule: Complete the business quality screens — circle of competence, moat existence, moat direction, pricing power, management integrity — before examining price multiples or valuation ratios; a business that fails qualitative screens must not be reconsidered solely because its price appears low
seeds.anti_scope: Does not mean price is unimportant or that it is the final consideration; means that price analysis is only valid and meaningful after business quality has been affirmatively confirmed
seeds.rationale: Buffett's Rule 6 states explicitly: lead analysis with business quality, not price. His evolution from Graham's cigar-butt approach was precisely the recognition that a wonderful company at a fair price beats a fair company at a wonderful price — a cheap price cannot rescue a structurally weak business over a long holding period.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A company should pay dividends only when it demonstrably cannot deploy retained capital at returns exceeding what owners could earn independently
seeds.qualitative_rule: Evaluate whether a dividend-paying company has genuinely exhausted high-return reinvestment opportunities; if the company is simultaneously paying regular dividends and earning high incremental returns on internally deployed capital, the capital allocation is sub-optimal and signals potential principal-agent misalignment
seeds.anti_scope: Does not disqualify dividend-paying companies from the investment universe; means dividend payment should be read as evidence about whether the business has run out of superior capital deployment options, not as a positive signal in itself
seeds.rationale: Buffett's capital allocation hierarchy places dividends as the last resort. His reasoning: if management can reinvest at returns exceeding shareholder opportunity cost, retained earnings compound faster inside the business. Berkshire has never paid a regular dividend because this condition has never been met.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The purchase price must imply an acceptable return even under a materially conservative, downside-case earnings scenario, not only under the base case
seeds.qualitative_rule: Run the valuation with a growth rate materially below the base case and confirm that the implied return at the purchase price remains acceptable relative to the risk-free alternative; if the investment thesis breaks under a plausible downside scenario, the price does not embed sufficient margin of safety
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require discounting all investments to Graham-era deep value levels; does not prohibit paying above-average multiples for exceptional businesses; requires only that the downside-case return remains acceptable before committing capital
seeds.rationale: Even within Buffett's evolved framework — where business quality provides a second layer of protection — there is always a price at which even the greatest business becomes a bad investment. In the Coca-Cola case, he specifically verified that a downside growth scenario still implied attractive returns at his purchase price before committing.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Management that demonstrably resists the institutional imperative — willing to hold excess cash, decline available acquisitions, and defy peer behavior when economics do not justify action — is a significant positive quality signal
seeds.qualitative_rule: Assess whether management has a documented pattern of declining available acquisitions when returns are insufficient, returning excess capital when no superior use exists, and resisting imitative pressure from industry peers; positive evidence includes past instances where management chose deliberate inaction over the pressure to deploy capital
seeds.anti_scope: Does not penalize management for making acquisitions per se; assesses specifically whether the pattern shows resistance to empire-building and peer imitation pressure as distinct from genuine opportunity-driven deployment
seeds.rationale: Buffett identified the institutional imperative — organizations' tendency to absorb available capital regardless of returns and reflexively imitate peers — as one of the most insidious forces in corporate governance. Managers who resist it are rare and command a meaningful quality premium in his analysis.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Every investment thesis must undergo explicit inversion analysis to enumerate plausible failure modes before any positive evaluation begins
seeds.qualitative_rule: Before constructing a bull case, analyst must produce a written enumeration of: ruin modes (permanent capital loss scenarios), critical dependency chains, fragility points under single shocks, incentive failure risks, and any single point of failure that could destroy the thesis independently
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require that all failure modes be eliminated before proceeding; only requires that each be explicitly identified and consciously accepted or rejected — the principle is not 'avoid all risk' but 'identify all risk'
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 1 from Munger's framework: inversion is the master principle from which most other principles derive. Directly attributable to Jacobi's maxim ('Invert, always invert') and operationalized in Munger's entry question: 'What would have to be true for this investment to be a catastrophic failure?' Skipping inversion means accepting an unexamined bull case, which is the primary source of investment catastrophe.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Any evidence of management dishonesty disqualifies the investment unconditionally
seeds.qualitative_rule: If management has demonstrated dishonesty in financial reporting, related-party dealings, public statements, or regulatory filings, the investment must be rejected regardless of all other factors including valuation, moat quality, or growth trajectory
seeds.anti_scope: Does not apply to management incompetence, strategic disagreements, or past errors of judgment; solely governs demonstrated integrity breaches — a poor capital allocator is not the same as a dishonest one
seeds.rationale: Munger held this as near-absolute: 'Never invest with dishonest management (no exceptions, ever).' He was explicitly more willing to forgive incompetence than dishonesty. His reasoning: a dishonest person running a business well will eventually steal or destroy value regardless of present performance, while a good person running a business poorly might improve.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The investment vehicle must never employ leverage that carries a maturity date, margin requirement, or any mechanism for forced liquidation
seeds.qualitative_rule: Any financing arrangement that introduces a non-zero probability of forced asset liquidation at an unfavorable time is prohibited; leverage instruments with no contractual call mechanism (analogous to insurance float) are permissible
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit investee companies from using operational leverage or float-based financing; applies exclusively to the investment vehicle's own capital structure — this is a self-constraint, not a screen on portfolio targets
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 2: 'Avoid anything that introduces a non-zero chance of forced liquidation; ruin is the one outcome compounding cannot repair.' Munger's aversion was mathematical, not conservative: leverage asymmetry means a temporary impairment becomes permanent when a margin call forces liquidation at the worst possible moment. He distinguished callable leverage (dangerous) from non-callable leverage like Berkshire's insurance float (acceptable).

seeds.qualitative_claim: Any investment where the source of competitive advantage cannot be explained through the analyst's lattice of mental models must be classified as unanalyzable and rejected
seeds.qualitative_rule: If the competitive advantage cannot be reduced to explanations drawing on models from psychology, economics, biology, physics, or other disciplines in the analyst's working lattice, the investment is placed in the too-hard pile; familiarity with the product or industry is insufficient if the advantage mechanism is opaque
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require understanding every technical or engineering detail of the product; requires only that the thesis — the source of advantage and path to value creation — be simple and explicable even when the underlying product is complex (BYD illustrates this boundary)
seeds.rationale: Munger's operationalization of circle of competence was specifically multidisciplinary: 'If the business cannot be reduced to a few reliable models you understand, put it in the too-hard pile.' This was not merely 'invest in what you know' — it was 'if you cannot explain the advantage mechanism through your model lattice, it is unanalyzable, not just unfamiliar.'

seeds.qualitative_claim: Before finalizing any investment decision, the analyst must explicitly audit the thesis for contamination by Munger's Standard Causes of Human Misjudgment
seeds.qualitative_rule: Analyst must check for at minimum: incentive-caused bias (who profits from recommending this?), social proof (is everyone else buying?), commitment-consistency bias (holding because already owned?), deprival super-reaction (buying because it dropped?), over-optimism, and availability bias; lollapalooza interactions among multiple biases must also be flagged
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require that detected biases automatically veto the investment; requires that each identified bias be explicitly named, its potential influence assessed, and the final recommendation state whether it was discounted — the investment may proceed if the underlying thesis survives independent of the bias
seeds.rationale: Munger's psychological framework from 'The Psychology of Human Misjudgment' treats the analyst's own cognition as the primary risk factor. A thesis that feels exciting or urgent is a red flag by definition. The profile factor psychological_bias_audit carries the second-highest weight in Munger's decision framework precisely because self-deception overwhelms most other analytical errors.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Market consensus or the investment activity of peers must never be used as positive evidence supporting an investment decision
seeds.qualitative_rule: If the primary positive evidence for an investment is that other investors are buying it, that broad consensus supports the thesis, or that the position is 'consensus long', the investment must be rejected on that basis alone — the bullish case must stand entirely on first-principles analysis
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit awareness of what other investors are doing; prohibits using their behavior as evidentiary support for the thesis — the distinction is between monitoring (allowed) and using social proof as a buy signal (prohibited)
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 2 explicitly prohibits: 'Never invest because others are investing (social proof).' Social proof is among the most dangerous biases in Munger's framework because it cascades: each investor treating others' behavior as evidence compounds the error across the market simultaneously, creating conditions for catastrophic collective misjudgment.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Management evaluation must center on the incentive structure rather than on stated character, reputation, or interview impressions
seeds.qualitative_rule: The compensation structure, promotion criteria, and performance metrics that management actually faces must be analyzed as the primary predictors of future behavior; character testimony, peer reputation, and analyst meetings are classified as supplementary and cannot override a misaligned incentive structure
seeds.anti_scope: Does not render character assessment irrelevant; it demotes character to a secondary confirmatory role — a well-designed incentive structure with good character is ideal, but incentive structure alone is the load-bearing variable
seeds.rationale: Strong Preference 2: 'Show me the incentive and I'll show you the outcome.' Munger trusted incentive structures more than character assessments as predictors. His reasoning: a good person in a bad incentive structure will eventually behave badly; a mediocre person in a well-designed structure will behave well. Compensation structure is 'a more reliable predictor than interviews, track records, or reputation.'

seeds.qualitative_claim: Competitive advantage must be structurally self-reinforcing through underlying economic mechanisms
seeds.qualitative_rule: Identify the specific positive feedback loop by which the competitive advantage compounds itself (e.g., scale → lower cost → market share → more scale); advantages sustained only by management excellence or favorable industry cycles must be classified as fragile and scored accordingly
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require that every moat be permanent or perfectly impervious; requires that a self-reinforcing economic mechanism be identifiable and articulable — the moat may narrow over time, but it must not depend solely on management effort to remain intact
seeds.rationale: Strong Preference 1 and the competitive_moat_durability profile factor both emphasize that moats erode by default and only survive when 'reinforced by economics, not just management effort.' Costco's membership model is the exemplar: the structural incentive (keep prices as low as possible) creates a feedback loop that management cannot easily abandon even if they wanted to.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Any investment thesis whose validity is contingent on a specific macroeconomic prediction being correct must be rejected
seeds.qualitative_rule: If the thesis requires a prediction about interest rates, currency movements, commodity prices, GDP trajectory, or credit cycle timing to be correct, the macroeconomic dependency must be treated as an unacceptable single point of failure and the investment rejected
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit awareness of macroeconomic context or scenario planning; prohibits constructing a thesis whose core value creation depends on a macro forecast — a business that performs well across macro scenarios is permissible; one that only works in a specific macro environment is not
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 2 states: 'Macro forecasts are usually twaddle masquerading as sophistication; they invite overconfidence without actionable edge.' Munger's anti-macro stance was epistemological — he believed the confidence investors place in macro predictions is itself a form of human misjudgment, not merely impractical. No one has demonstrated systematic macro forecasting ability.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A business must demonstrate the structural ability to raise prices in excess of input cost increases without meaningful customer attrition as a primary test of franchise quality
seeds.qualitative_rule: Evaluate historical pricing behavior during documented periods of input cost pressure; if the business has systematically absorbed cost increases rather than passing them to customers, classify as lacking pricing power; if no such historical period exists, assess structural reasons why customers would accept price increases
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require that pricing power be exercised frequently or aggressively; requires only that the structural ability exists — a business may deliberately choose not to raise prices (Costco) while still possessing the power to do so
seeds.rationale: Strong Preference 1 identifies pricing power as Munger's 'acid test' for business quality, derived directly from the See's Candies experience. The ability to raise prices above cost inflation is the clearest empirical demonstration that customers value the product beyond its commodity equivalent and that the business possesses genuine franchise value.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A business with unfavorable long-term structural economics must be rejected regardless of apparent cheapness on near-term financial metrics
seeds.qualitative_rule: If the business's structural economics — return on incremental capital, competitive trajectory, pricing power trend — are deteriorating or fundamentally unattractive, low P/E, P/B, or EV/EBITDA multiples do not constitute a sufficient investment rationale; the investment must be rejected
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit investing in currently low-profitability businesses where structural economics are improving; only prohibits investing in businesses with persistently poor long-term economics solely on the grounds that the price appears cheap relative to current earnings
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 2 explicitly prohibits: 'Never invest in businesses with unfavorable long-term economics just because they are cheap.' This represents Munger's decisive break from Graham cigar-butt investing. The mathematics are asymmetric: a business compounding at poor returns over decades overwhelms any initial valuation discount, while the opportunity cost of holding destroys value twice.

seeds.qualitative_claim: When a bull case requires multiple independent assumptions to simultaneously hold true, the combined probability of success must be explicitly calculated rather than intuitively estimated
seeds.qualitative_rule: List all assumptions the bull case depends on; when count exceeds threshold, calculate the combined probability as the product of individual probabilities; if any single assumption is not independently verifiable, flag as fragile and document the fragility explicitly in the investment memo
seeds.anti_scope: Does not prohibit investments with multiple assumptions; requires that the conjunction fallacy be explicitly corrected — intuitive probability estimates for compound conditions are systematically too high and must be replaced with an explicit calculation
seeds.rationale: Inversion protocol from Iron Law 1: 'If the thesis requires three independent assumptions to all hold, the probability of success is the product of the three individual probabilities — often much lower than intuition suggests.' This is a direct application of the conjunction fallacy from Munger's psychology of human misjudgment framework. Over-optimism about compound conditions is among the most reliable sources of investment error.

seeds.qualitative_claim: A superior business generates cash substantially in excess of what is required merely to maintain its competitive position
seeds.qualitative_rule: Separately estimate maintenance capex (spending required to prevent competitive erosion) and growth capex (spending on incremental opportunities with positive returns); businesses where maintenance capex consumes the majority of operating earnings are classified as capital intensity traps and scored unfavorably
seeds.anti_scope: Does not penalize businesses that reinvest heavily for growth at attractive returns; only penalizes businesses that reinvest heavily to avoid decline without generating incremental return — high reinvestment is good when it earns excess returns, bad when it merely sustains existing returns
seeds.rationale: Strong Preference 1 makes an explicit distinction: 'Businesses that must reinvest heavily merely to avoid decline (maintenance capex traps) are fundamentally different from businesses that reinvest heavily because they have excellent opportunities. Munger disliked the former, not the latter.' This distinction is critical for evaluating true free cash flow quality versus reported earnings.

seeds.qualitative_claim: Post-mortem analysis of investment errors must identify the specific reasoning failure in the analyst's own judgment
seeds.qualitative_rule: When an investment underperforms materially or fails, the post-mortem must name the specific error in reasoning — which assumption was wrong, which bias was operative, which signal was misread — rather than attributing the loss to market conditions, macroeconomic shocks, or bad luck; the learning is invalid until a specific judgment failure is named
seeds.anti_scope: Does not deny that external events can cause losses independent of analytical error; requires that alongside any external cause, the analyst also identify what they failed to anticipate, why, and what class of future error this reveals — external causes and analytical failures are not mutually exclusive
seeds.rationale: Iron Law 3 on intellectual honesty: Munger 'did not blame the market, the economy, or bad luck. He identified his specific error in judgment and tried to learn from it.' This is the operational implementation of radical intellectual honesty. Attribution to external causes forecloses learning; attribution to internal reasoning failures enables the systematic improvement that compounds over time.

seeds.qualitative_claim: The business model's structural incentives must make serving customers well the path to maximum profitability
seeds.qualitative_rule: Identify whether the business model structurally rewards customer value creation (membership models, subscription models with genuine switching costs, distribution scale) or structurally rewards extraction from customers (opaque fee structures, asymmetric information exploitation, lock-in without value delivery); the latter is classified as inherently fragile
seeds.anti_scope: Does not require management to be altruistic or indifferent to profit; requires only that the structural path to maximum profitability for the business coincides with genuine customer benefit — businesses where management must choose between profits and customers are structurally inferior to those where the choice does not arise
seeds.rationale: Strong Preference 1 identifies incentive alignment as a distinct quality criterion beyond moat analysis: 'Are the structural incentives of the business model aligned so that serving customers well is also the path to maximum profitability? (Costco's membership model is the exemplar.)' Munger viewed misaligned incentive structures as both ethically unacceptable and pragmatically fragile — customer extraction models invite regulatory response and competitive displacement.

seeds.qualitative_claim: 无法用一句话向外行说清楚公司如何赚钱，则禁止投资该公司
seeds.qualitative_rule: 投资分析开始前，必须能将商业模式压缩为一句话；否则立即终止分析流程，不进入后续任何评估环节
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束能力圈之外公司的学习型研究和跟踪阅读，仅约束资金投入决策
seeds.rationale: 段永平最高频铁律'不懂不做'，且其'看得懂'标准是可以一句话说清楚给外行听，明确排除'大概看得懂'式的自欺欺人；这是六步分析序列的第一关
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反

seeds.qualitative_claim: 理解其竞争力需要预测底层科学突破或前沿技术路线的行业，不得投资
seeds.qualitative_rule: 若判断公司护城河依赖对生物医药研发管线、半导体制程演进等前沿科学路线图的正确预测，则拒绝投资
seeds.anti_scope: 不排除科技消费品投资，前提是投资者具备一线产品使用与行业竞争的直接认知，而非依赖科学路线预测
seeds.rationale: 段永平明确不投生物医药研发管线和半导体制程，理由是无法判断科学突破的时间窗口；Apple例外因其具备手机行业一线经营与竞争经验，买的是消费者心智而非技术路线判断
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反

seeds.qualitative_claim: 投资资金必须全部来自自有资金，禁止使用任何形式的借贷杠杆
seeds.qualitative_rule: 投资组合中任何仓位不得由借贷资金支撑；若存在借贷融资，需强制清除至零杠杆
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束以自有资金为担保的卖Put操作；不约束被投企业经营层面的合理债务融资
seeds.rationale: 段永平铁律原话：'借钱会让你失去等待权'——杠杆在市场最非理性、最不该卖出时逼迫清仓，从根本上破坏价值投资赖以生效的时间前提
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反

seeds.qualitative_claim: 存在欺骗消费者、投资者或合作伙伴的明确历史记录的企业，不得投资
seeds.qualitative_rule: 若公司历史上存在财务造假、系统性虚假承诺或蓄意欺骗行为的可信证据，则禁止投资
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束管理层个人能力不足或经营失误；不要求管理层历史上从未犯过任何错误；仅约束诚信层面的根本性、系统性问题
seeds.rationale: 段永平将'本分'定为最高级决策过滤器，'明知是错的事情就不做'是其核心定义。企业文化一旦腐化，再好的商业模式也会出问题，且这类风险极难被财务数据提前发现
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反

seeds.qualitative_claim: 需要依赖精密财务模型才能得出'便宜'结论的投资机会，不具备足够的安全边际
seeds.qualitative_rule: 若分析流程依赖DCF或多情景精确建模才能判断估值合理，应将安全边际评级下调；真正合格的买入机会应以常识即可判断便宜与否
seeds.anti_scope: 不排除使用财务数据作辅助性确认；不约束定性分析中引用财务比率验证方向；仅约束以精密模型作为估值依据的必要性
seeds.rationale: 段永平直接语录：'如果你需要用Excel来算，那说明这个投资机会不够好。'精确模型的输入假设本质上是猜测，与其追求精确的错误，不如接受模糊的正确
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/模块-5失败案例与自我修正/模块-6差异化边界/模块-7行为准则与自检清单/模块-8典型语言风格/高频语录与使用语境

seeds.qualitative_claim: 价格下跌不构成卖出信号，只有投资论点发生实质性破裂才是合法卖出依据
seeds.qualitative_rule: 每次卖出决策必须附有明确的论点破裂陈述；不得以'股价下跌'或'亏损止损'作为卖出的唯一理由
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束因初次判断错误、论点已被证伪而快速离场的操作；此原则仅约束恐慌性、无论点依据的卖出
seeds.rationale: 段永平：'如果明天跌五成，你会加仓还是想卖？如果想卖，说明一开始就不该买。'价格波动不改变内在价值，恐慌性卖出是价值投资者最常见的自毁行为
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/模块-5失败案例与自我修正/模块-6差异化边界/模块-7行为准则与自检清单/段永平的决策问话清单

seeds.qualitative_claim: 买入决策应以愿意在市场长期封闭情况下仍能安心持有为心理前提
seeds.qualitative_rule: 买入前须通过心理测试：假设此后市场长期无法交易，仍会选择持有，才可执行买入；对短期流动性有依赖的仓位不得建立
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束因论点破裂而提前退出的操作；不要求持有期必须达到特定年限，仅约束买入时的心理预期框架设置
seeds.rationale: 段永平经典语录：'如果你不愿意持有一家公司十年，那你一分钟也不要持有。'以封闭市场的极端假设检验买入决策，过滤掉依赖短期市场流动性的投机性买入
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/模块-5失败案例与自我修正/模块-6差异化边界/模块-7行为准则与自检清单/段永平的决策问话清单

seeds.qualitative_claim: 目标企业的核心品牌应在其品类中占据消费者联想的首选位置
seeds.qualitative_rule: 护城河评估须验证：消费者提及该品类时，目标品牌是否自然浮现为首选——以心智联想而非市场份额为判断依据
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束非品牌型企业（B2B供应链、基础设施类等）；不要求所有投资标的均为消费品牌
seeds.rationale: 段永平以茅台（中国白酒心智首选）和Apple（智能手机生态心智锁定）为核心案例：心智份额是护城河的最深层体现，给竞争对手大量资金也无法在短期内打掉
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/案例-3茅台约-2013-2014逆向买入的本土洞察

seeds.qualitative_claim: 管理层资本配置决策应以股东长期利益最大化为导向，而非以短期业绩表现或自身利益为导向
seeds.qualitative_rule: 评估管理层历史决策模式：是否在合理时机回购或分红，薪酬水平是否合理，是否克制避免无关并购——以行为记录而非公开声明为判断依据
seeds.anti_scope: 不要求企业必须执行回购或分红；不适用于优质再投资机会充足的高成长期企业；不约束合理战略性并购
seeds.rationale: 段永平在Apple和茅台的持仓追踪中均将'管理层资本配置行为'列为持续监控指标，'管理层是在为股东着想吗'是其评估管理层本分的核心检验问题
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/案例-2苹果2011产品人的优势

seeds.qualitative_claim: 好的商业模式在平庸管理者接手后仍应能维持正常盈利运营
seeds.qualitative_rule: 评估商业模式时须问：若现任团队全部离职，普通职业经理人接手后公司能否维持盈利——若必须依赖特定天才级人物则降低商业模式质量评分
seeds.anti_scope: 不要求经营完全不依赖管理能力；不约束处于创业早期、高度依赖创始人的公司之初期评估阶段
seeds.rationale: 段永平经典语录：'好的商业模式是傻瓜都能经营的生意——因为迟早会有傻瓜来经营。'他本人离开步步高后OPPO/vivo持续高速增长，是此原则最直接的亲身实证
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/案例-2苹果2011产品人的优势

seeds.qualitative_claim: 护城河评估中应验证企业是否具备涨价而不流失核心客户的能力
seeds.qualitative_rule: 评估护城河时须追问历史上是否存在成功涨价且核心客户粘性未显著下降的案例；若定价权缺失，需降低护城河评级
seeds.anti_scope: 不要求企业必须已实施涨价；不适用于处于渗透率扩张阶段、以价换量为战略的早期企业
seeds.rationale: 段永平以茅台为典型：'涨价后消费者不但不减少购买，反而更想买——因为价格本身就是品牌价值的一部分。'定价权是护城河深度的最直接行为实证
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/案例-3茅台约-2013-2014逆向买入的本土洞察

seeds.qualitative_claim: 判断出现实质性错误时应立即认错并退出，不得以当前账面亏损状态为由拖延清仓
seeds.qualitative_rule: 每次持仓检视中，若原始投资论点已被明确证伪，必须启动退出流程，不受当前账面盈亏状态影响
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束对负面信息的持续核实过程；不约束短期信息噪音引发的情绪性判断；仅约束论点已被明确证伪后的拖延行为
seeds.rationale: 段永平将'知错就改'纳入'本分'铁律：'买错了就认，认了就卖，不抱着错误不放。'损失厌恶驱动的持仓拖延是价值毁灭最常见的行为根源
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反

seeds.qualitative_claim: 投资决策不得依赖宏观经济预测或市场短期方向判断作为核心触发依据
seeds.qualitative_rule: 分析框架中不得将宏观走向或市场时机预测列为关键投资触发条件；分析聚焦点应为公司基本面本身
seeds.anti_scope: 不排除了解宏观环境作为行业背景知识；不约束逆向投资中对市场极度悲观情绪的识别和利用
seeds.rationale: 段永平直接表述：'我不看宏观，也不预测市场。我只关心这家公司本身。'茅台买入时他做的是消费结构分析而非政策走向预测，这一区别具有方法论意义
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/案例-3茅台约-2013-2014逆向买入的本土洞察

seeds.qualitative_claim: 分析流程中，商业模式和企业文化质量的判断必须先于估值评估
seeds.qualitative_rule: 分析顺序必须严格遵循：生意质量→管理层质量→价格；若前两关未通过，禁止进入估值讨论环节
seeds.anti_scope: 不约束以低估值为初筛线索的机会发现阶段；初筛后仍需按正确顺序完整验证前两关
seeds.rationale: 段永平框架核心语录：'right business, right people, right price'——三个词的顺序代表严格优先级，生意优于人，人优于价格，这是他与'捡烟蒂'式价值投资的根本性分野
seeds.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/模块-4决策推理链/模块-5失败案例与自我修正/模块-6差异化边界/模块-7行为准则与自检清单/模块-8典型语言风格/高频语录与使用语境

seed.qualitative_rule: 投资组合中不得持有任何形式的做空头寸
seed.anti_scope: 不约束以卖Put形式表达的正向看多操作；不约束组合层面的合理对冲工具使用
seed.rationale: 段永平铁律：即使判断方向正确，市场非理性持续时间无法预测，做空的时间窗口不可控且理论损失无上限，与价值投资所依赖的确定性前提根本不兼容
seed.supporting_section_id: duan/y-大师灵魂文档-段永平-duan-yongping/模块-1身份与起源/模块-2投资哲学演变/模块-3核心原则体系/铁律从不违反
